rag_rules: Preserve the AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks only in valid component type or implementation scopes
Use component type annexes for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equation declarations
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation annexes after subcomponents and connections
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
generic_rules: Maintain the authoritative source of component names, ports, directions, data types, subcomponents, and connections in the input AADL model
Preserve the original AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Ensure that AGREE annex blocks are inserted in the correct scope for the target component or implementation
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use exact Boolean expressions only when the port is an AGREE bool in scope; otherwise omit the unsafe clause
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless the model exposes explicit typed ports or constants for them
anti_patterns: Do not insert AGREE annex blocks in invalid scopes
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Do not encode AADL property names as AGREE variables without explicit typed ports or constants
irrelevant_topics: AGREE, annex, assume, guarantee, assign, validator, or repair
